decision.title: Payload + Supabase + Vercel
decision.summary: Use Payload CMS (with-vercel-website template) on Vercel with PostgreSQL hosted on Supabase. Swap template DB env from Neon to Supabase connection string when implementing.
decision.impact: All schema and migrations target Postgres via Payload; Blob/storage may still use Vercel Blob or move to Supabase Storage in a later decision.

decision.title: Where internal process documentation lives
decision.summary: Business process docs stay internal: private Git repo (preferred for `.planning/workflows/`), optional admin-only Payload collections, or private Notion/Confluence. Public repo must not hold confidential playbooks; use a private repo or redact.
decision.impact: Sets expectation that CRM + website are separate from SOP storage; secrets never in git.

decision.title: CRM / marketing hub: EngageBay vs HubSpot
decision.summary: Open. Compare automation, email, deals, forms, API/webhooks, and cost. All staff use the **same CRM account (one org/tenant)**; users may use **different email domains**—CRMs invite by address, domain alignment not required. Integrate site/forms so contacts and pipeline are managed in CRM; Payload remains CMS for public content unless you duplicate lean copies.
decision.impact: Defines sync direction for contacts, sequences, and whether forms post to CRM first vs Payload; team onboarding via CRM invites regardless of @domain.

decision.title: Shared passwords and API keys for third-party tools
decision.summary: Requirement acknowledged: team needs one place to view/maintain logins for other systems. Do not store plaintext in content collections without an encryption/security review. Options: team password manager (1Password/Bitwarden), Payload encrypted fields + RBAC, or dedicated secrets patterns.
decision.impact: May remain process + external tool for v1; in-app vault only after threat model.
